creative_brief: Clean, warm layout. Hero image: a real product label showing farm origin (e.g. Bundarra Farm pork belly). Earthy palette — greens and cream. Single CTA button mid-lower body linking to the FarmThru store. No investment messaging.
subject: You deserve to know who grew your food
preheader: Last Tuesday, a mum in Brookvale asked me one question I couldn't stop thinking about.
body:
Last Tuesday at our Brookvale hub, a mum picking up her weekly order held up a pack of Bundarra Farm pork belly and asked her daughter: "Do you know where this came from?"

Her daughter shook her head.

So the mum pointed to the label — Bundarra Farm, NSW — and said, "That's a real place. Real people raised this for us."

I watched from behind the counter and thought: that's the whole point.

You shouldn't need a degree in supply chains to know what you're feeding your family. But right now, most of what fills supermarket shelves has travelled 2,400km through warehouses and wholesalers — and the farmer's name never makes it to the pack.

That's the problem FarmThru exists to fix. One problem. One mission: you deserve to know exactly who grew your food, and so do your kids.

We're a farm-direct grocery store. You order online. You collect from our Brookvale hub, Monday to Friday. No middlemen. No warehouses. No mystery.

Every product traces back to a real farm you can look up tonight. Paris Creek Farms in South Australia — carbon-neutral biodynamic dairy. Collins Wild Caught off the South Coast of NSW. Farmer Brown eggs. Rachel's Farm in Kempsey. Around 15 to 20 partner farms and suppliers across NSW and beyond, each one chosen because we'd feed it to our own families.

You're not locked into a subscription. No membership fees. You order when you need to, collect when it suits you.

This isn't about premium grocery shopping. It's about a basic right — knowing what's on your plate and who put it there.

If that matters to you, start here. Browse the store, read the farm stories, and place your first order when you're ready.

→ See where your food comes from at FarmThru.com.au

No commitment. No lock-in. Just food you can actually trace.

Rachel & the FarmThru team
